title: cytisus ratisbonensis var. microphyllus
intro: Cytisus ratisbonensis var. microphyllus, also known as Dwarf Austrian Pea, is a compact, shrub-like legume that belongs to the Fabaceae family. The medicinal properties of this plant include its ability to act as a diuretic, helping to reduce water retention and alleviate symptoms of edema, as well as its potential to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Cytisus ratisbonensis var. microphyllus include its use in treating urinary tract issues, such as kidney stones and incontinence, as well as its potential to support cardiovascular health and reduce the risk of certain cancers. Cytisus ratisbonensis var. microphyllus can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional folk medicine, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
